authorization.statement: Please proceed for completion by 4:00 pm 07/22/26, Deliver in ACAD and PDF $600.00, Existing Main Level As-Built Floor Plan.

scope.deliverable: Existing Main Level As-Built Floor Plan
scope.drafting_basis: Point cloud and supporting information provided by BDPC
included: One Existing Main Level As-Built Floor Plan
Existing walls, partitions, rooms, doors, windows, openings, stairs, fixed fixtures/equipment, and observed architectural context where supported
Confirmed dimensions, labels, notes, room names, title block, layers, styles, layout, viewport, page setup, and CTB/STB when supplied
Internal geometry, dependency, annotation, native-open, and plot QA
Controlled delivery package containing one native AutoCAD DWG and one PDF
excluded: Design work, alternates, demolition plans, or space planning
Other floors, elevations, sections, roof plans, reflected ceiling plans, details, BIM/3D modeling, rendering, or schedules
Field survey, certified as-built measurement, scan-registration certification, permit submission, or professional certification
Additional assignments, added sheets, design changes, fragmented reviews, or revisions based on new or changed inputs

commercial.payment_requirement: Payment will be provided after delivery per current agreement; payment is not a pre-production blocker.
commercial.minor_correction: One consolidated minor correction pass limited to a demonstrable drafting error or omission, returned within two business days
commercial.additional_services: $90/hour or a separately authorized fixed/day rate

schedule.clock_start_rule: Production may begin after written authorization, controlling inputs, target AutoCAD and BDPC standards confirmation, licensed compatible runtime access, and a locked production window. Payment is due after delivery under the current agreement.

activation_gates.name: $600 payment
activation_gates.evidence: Current agreement: payment will be provided after delivery.

activation_gates.name: Controlling point cloud and support package
activation_gates.evidence: Thomas CAD is organized by scope; 930 media/CAD/point-cloud files were classified; primary/support LAS and trace references are identified.

activation_gates.name: AutoCAD version, title block, CTB/STB, fonts, xrefs, and BDPC standards
activation_gates.evidence: Kickoff transcript confirms 1/2-inch precision, 3.5-inch wall baseline, TCADD floor-plan logic, 1419 paper-space/title-block standard, and floor-plate scale anchor.

activation_gates.name: Licensed compatible AutoCAD runtime
activation_gates.evidence: AutoCAD/Core Console trace-reference save succeeded, but final production and plotting still require licensed AutoCAD workflow.

activation_gates.name: Locked production day
activation_gates.evidence: Delivery directed for 4:00 PM EDT on July 22, 2026

work_plan.status: complete: primary/support scans, crawl, slices, trace DWG/DXF, and scope register prepared
blocked until AutoCAD license: trace manually from prepared master DWG/DXF

work_plan.workstream: Openings, rooms, content, dimensions, and notes
work_plan.status: blocked until drafting; room names, door swings, windows, fixtures need confirmation or disclosure

work_plan.workstream: Native QA, plot QA, and delivery package
Managed reserve and minor correction allowance
work_plan.status: reserved only for documented in-scope issue

standards_required: Layer, text, dimension, and annotation standards
Required fonts, shapes, xrefs, and object dependencies
File naming, layout, viewport, page setup, and issue requirements
Required room names and known source limitations
automation_controls: Scripts, point-cloud processing, AutoCAD APIs, AI-assisted visual analysis, and repeatable QA may accelerate production
All generated geometry and deliverables remain subject to human review
Automation does not replace BDPC architectural authority, source authority, or final professional acceptance
Source files remain protected; production uses controlled working copies
Unsupported dimensions, room names, openings, wall construction, and concealed conditions are not invented
acceptance_criteria: The package contains one Existing Main Level As-Built Floor Plan only, in native AutoCAD DWG and PDF formats
The DWG opens in the confirmed target version with no known production-critical dependency failure
The plan reflects the controlling point cloud and supporting information without proposed design or unsupported conditions
Walls and closed boundaries have no unexplained gaps, duplicates, or unreviewed candidate geometry
Doors, windows, openings, stairs, fixtures, and fixed equipment are placed where supported or disclosed as unresolved
Typical framed walls use the confirmed 3.5-inch baseline unless evidence supports a different assembly.
Dimensions, labels, room names, and notes are source-supported or clearly qualified
Dimensions display no finer than the confirmed 1/2-inch residential convention and chained/overall dimensions reconcile.
The PDF is legible, correctly framed, free of clipping, and consistent with the final visible DWG
Material unresolved conditions or approved departures are disclosed rather than concealed
change_control: Proposed plan, site work, or design changes
New or materially changed inputs after production begins
Point-cloud registration or transformation beyond the confirmed basis
Reconstruction of materially missing conditions or field verification

evidence_snapshot.registration_boundary: Five native-coordinate scan pairs were analyzed: four showed weak overlap and one showed no material overlap. No transform, accepted registration tolerance, or survey-grade registration claim was adopted.

truth_boundary: The current authority is one Existing Main Level As-Built Floor Plan in native AutoCAD DWG and PDF. Pre-AutoCAD discovery, source organization, image contact sheets, point-cloud slices, trace DWG/DXF references, and kickoff-derived drafting rules are prepared. BDPC retains architectural authority, interpretation, code and permit responsibility, and final professional acceptance.
